adds.rationale: Frontline (NYSE: FRO) fills the geopolitical-energy slot the profile explicitly names (tactical tanker shipping); Houthi Red Sea attacks since November 2023 are forcing rerouting via Cape of Good Hope, stretching ton-mile demand and sustaining VLCC rates well above Frontline's cash breakeven, while tight newbuild supply (new VLCC deliveries nearly halved year-on-year) limits any near-term rate reversal.

news_evidence.summary: S&P Global Commodity Insights: 'Tighter fleet capacity, geopolitical issues, longer voyages to boost 2024 tanker freight' — Red Sea crisis and Cape of Good Hope rerouting expected to drive elevated VLCC freight rates throughout 2024, with new VLCC deliveries having almost halved year-on-year.

Investing.com: Frontline Q4 2023 earnings call — 'Best year we've had in 15 years'; Q1 2024 forward bookings already 81% of VLCC days at $55,100/day vs. cash breakeven of $28,800/day; cash position $416M, no debt maturities until 2027, fleet average age 5.8 years with 99% ECO-compliant vessels.

removes.rationale: NuScale's commercial thesis has materially broken: its only U.S. project (UAMPS Carbon Free Power Project, six 77-MW SMRs in Idaho) was terminated in November 2023 for lack of utility subscribers, and Q1 2024 results confirmed accelerating deterioration with revenue of only $1.4M against $5.71M consensus, a net loss of $48.1M, and no new customer signings visible on the horizon.

news_evidence.summary: E&E News by POLITICO: 'NuScale cancels first-of-a-kind nuclear project as costs surge' — UAMPS and NuScale mutually terminated the Carbon Free Power Project in November 2023 after utilities failed to subscribe enough power, removing the company's only near-term revenue pathway despite $232M in DOE cost-share backing.

Yahoo Finance: 'NuScale Power Corp (SMR) Reports Q1 2024 Earnings: Misses Revenue and Earnings Estimates' — Q1 2024 revenue $1.4M vs. $5.71M expected (a 75% miss); net loss $48.1M widening from $35.6M a year earlier; cash $137M at current burn rate.

Investing.com / NuScale Q1 2024 earnings call: management cited NRC-certified design and Romania FEED Phase 2 study as progress markers but acknowledged zero new U.S. customer signings and dependency on two unconfirmed congressional appropriations totaling $900M; the company launched a $50-60M annualized cost-savings initiative — a signal of distress, not commercial momentum.

rationale_overall: This rebalance executes one swap: SMR (NuScale) out, FRO (Frontline) in. NuScale's original thesis — earliest-mover commercial SMR deployment in the U.S. — suffered a decisive blow when UAMPS terminated the Idaho CFPP in November 2023, and Q1 2024 results confirmed the revenue collapse ($1.4M actual vs. $5.71M expected) with no visible customer recovery; the nuclear fission wave remains intact thematically but NuScale is its weakest single-stock expression at this moment. Frontline fills the geopolitical-energy slot the profile explicitly names (tactical tanker/LNG shipping) and carries a concrete near-term catalyst: Red Sea Houthi disruptions have sustained VLCC day rates at approximately double Frontline's cash breakeven through Q1 2024, with tight fleet supply extending the tailwind. The post-change watchlist covers AI (GOOGL, MSFT, NVDA), robotics (BOTZ), quantum (IONQ), rockets (RKLB), defense-rearmament (ITA), and geopolitical-energy (FRO) — seven distinct thematic buckets across 8 tickers, with the nuclear bucket now vacant pending a better-positioned entry (a nuclear ETF or uranium miner) at a future rebalance.
